title: Group Data Analyst
description: Group Data Analyst Not Disclosed Mt.Hope/Curepe Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 13/02/2026 Human Resource Officer Log in or register to apply Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save We are seeking a Group Data Analyst to provide support at a group-level high-quality data analysis, BI reporting, and insights for reporting and decision-making. CAREER OPPORTUNITY GROUP DATA ANALYST We are a major part of the Food Manufacturing sector and serve our Regional, North American and European markets. OUR NEED We are seeking a Group Data Analyst to provide support at a group-level high-quality data analysis, BI reporting, and insights for reporting and decision-making. The role focuses on building and maintaining dashboards, analysing data across multiple entities, ensuring data accuracy, and driving improvements in reporting, automation, and data processes across the group. KEY DUTIES Included among the duties and responsibilities are: - BI Reporting & Data Analysis – Develop, maintain and enhance BI Reports and extract and analyse data from multiple systems. Data Quality & Governance – Perform data validation checks and investigate anomalies and document data process and controls. Shareholder Support & Insight – Partner with all stakeholders to understand reporting needs and respond with insights to impact on decision making. Systems & Process Improvement – Contribute to system enhancement initiatives, support reporting solutions and identify continuous data improvement flows. DESIRED BACKGROUND/SKILLS The incumbent should possess : Bachelor’s Degree in Data Analytics, Business, Finance, IT, or a related field. Experience creating and analysing reports in Power BI (or similar BI tools). 3+ years’ experience in data analysis, reporting, or BI-focused roles. 3+ years’ experience working across multiple systems or entities is an advantage. Strong analytical and data interpretation skills, attention to detail with a structured, methodical approach. Ability to work with large, complex data sets, strong Excel skills and experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI). Understanding of accounting principles and group reporting. Ability to communicate financial information clearly to non-finance stakeholders. Strong time management skills with the ability to meet deadlines. If this sounds like the place for you and you think that you have what it takes to excel in this role, please forward your Résumé by February 28, 2026 . Kindly note that only suitable candidates will be contacted. Ref: BGLGRDATAC1 Log in or register to apply

title: Data Steward
description: Data Steward S.M. Jaleel & Company Limited San Fernando Not disclosed Permanent full-time Updated 12/02/2026 HR Department Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save The Data Steward is responsible for managing all data during the full life cycle. Also, with ERP group implementations and on-going support inclusive of business requirement analysis and technical design for the SMJ group. Position Overview The Data Steward is responsible for managing all data during the full life cycle. Also, with ERP group implementations and on-going support inclusive of business requirement analysis and technical design for the SMJ group. Key Responsibilities Master Data Creation & Maintenance Create and maintain Indirect (Garage/Factory/Properties/HSE) codes in line with Group standards. Accurately capture and update Indirect codes on Epicor. Create and maintain Supplier codes in accordance with Group data governance policies. Accurately capture Supplier codes on Epicor ensuring completeness and correctness. Capture and maintain Raw Material codes on Epicor in line with approved standards. Maintain Customer Master Files ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and consistency. Manage Finished Goods (FG) pricing updates and ensure pricing accuracy in the system. Ensure all master data entries comply with company policies and data standards. Maintain proper documentation for all master data changes. Data Governance & Compliance Conduct regular data governance audits to ensure data integrity and compliance with Group policies Identify data inconsistencies and implement corrective actions Ensure adherence to master data standards and approval workflows. Provide audit reports highlighting data quality issues and improvement actions. Support internal and external audit requirements relating to master data. System Data Management (Epicor) Ensure timely and accurate entry of all approved master data into Epicor. Monitor data quality within the system and proactively resolve discrepancies. Collaborate with IT and relevant departments to improve system data processes. Support testing and validation of system updates affecting master data. Stakeholder Support & Continuous Improvement Provide guidance to internal stakeholders on data standards and code creation processes. Respond to data-related queries and resolve issues within agreed service levels. Recommend process improvements to enhance data accuracy and efficiency. Support cross-functional teams in maintaining high-quality master data standards across the organization. Ethics & Confidentiality Uphold organizational standards and maintain strict confidentiality at all times. Education, Qualifications and knowledge requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Data Science or a related field. Certified Data Management (CDMP) - Associate or Practitioner level. Data & Information Governance Course – Trinidad & Tobago. Collibra Data Steward Certification. International ICT Governance Certifications – ISO 27001, SOC 2, CSA STAR. Minimum of five (5) years of experience in Infrastructure/Systems Administrator Epicor full life cycle implementations experience. Experience in a multi-site environment preferred. Strong knowledge of IT operational processes, resource management, and performance monitoring. Excellent critical thinking, problem-solving, decision-making, and organizational abilities. Strong communication, interpersonal and collaboration skills. High level of integrity, adaptability, and results orientation. Ref: Data Steward Apply Now

title: S.M. Jaleel & Company Limited
description: S.M. Jaleel & Company Limited In 1924, S.M. Jaleel and Co. Ltd. from South Trinidad began making soft drinks. Four decades later, S.M. Jaleel and Co. successfully competes in the Caribbean market with soft drink giants such as Coca Cola and Pepsi and holds the spot of number one distributor of soft drinks and fruit drinks in the Caribbean. Today the values and standards we worked with as a family owned Company, are the same values and standards that serve as a multi-national entity. The seven brands that make up the SM Jaleel family are marketed in over 50 countries worldwide, making our seven brands of non-alcoholic, beverages household names throughout the Caribbean. S.M. Jaleel and Company Limited owns and operates Factory Sites and Distribution Companies in Jamaica, Barbados, St. Lucia, Guyana and Saudi Arabia. Email Me Jobs From This Recruiter

title: GROUP ANALYST – BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE (ACADO LIMITED)
description: GROUP ANALYST – BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE (ACADO LIMITED) Agostini Limited San Juan/Barataria Not disclosed Not disclosed Updated 12/02/2026 HR Apply Now Share Share this job Facebook Twitter Report This Job Save Save The Group Analyst – Business Intelligence collaborates with stakeholders and departments across Acado and Agostini Group companies in all regions to address business challenges by harnessing business intelligence platforms and integrated data solutions. Position Summary: The Group Analyst – Business Intelligence collaborates with stakeholders and departments across Acado and Agostini Group companies in all regions to address business challenges by harnessing business intelligence platforms and integrated data solutions. Job Responsibilities: Requirements Gathering: Collaborate with stakeholders to elicit, validate, and prioritize data and reporting needs, ensuring business intelligence solutions are aligned with organizational objectives. Process Mapping & Redesign: Document current data collection and reporting processes (“as-is”), and design future-state workflows (“to-be”) that optimize the use of BI tools and platforms. Gap Analysis: Assess gaps between business intelligence requirements and existing data infrastructure or reporting capabilities. Recommend enhancements, integrations or process improvements to bridge these gaps. BI Solution Ownership: Serve as the subject-matter expert for BI platforms and reporting modules, such as Power BI, ensuring optimal utilization and continuous improvement across departments. Report & Dashboard Design: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports that transform complex data into actionable insights for decision-makers. Integration Coordination: Define functional requirements for integrating BI solutions with other business applications (e.g., ERP, CRM, WMS, POS), ensuring seamless data flow and consolidated reporting. Configuration Support: Partner with business teams to configure BI tools, set up data fields, workflows, and access roles, maintaining security and data integrity. Data Quality & Migration Readiness: Establish data models, cleansing rules, validation criteria, and migration plans to ensure accuracy, consistency, and reliability of BI outputs. Testing: Develop and execute test scenarios, scripts, and acceptance criteria for BI solutions; coordinate system integration testing (SIT), user acceptance testing (UAT), and manage defect resolution. Position Requirements: A Bachelor’s degree in Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Management Information Systems or any relevant combination of education and experience. A background in Finance or Accounting would be an asset. Advanced certification in Power BI. At least three (3) years’ experience in a Business Intelligence Officer or a Business Analyst role. In depth knowledge of BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, SQL and SSAS. Other Skills/Attributes required: Ability to undertake research into the industry sector, markets and competitors. Ability to collect, analyze and interpret data and statistics. Developing, advising on and implementing agreed solutions. Exercises sound independent judgement within established guidelines. Communicates in a highly persuasive manner (orally and written). Proven track record of delivering in time-pressured environments. Excellent communication and relationship building skills. Self-starter with strong self-management skills. Ability to organize and manage multiple priorities. Ref: GrpAnaBusIntgcccccc Apply Now
